summary: Hotz argues that the real power structure in America is an interconnected oligarchy spanning government, corporations, media, NGOs, and intelligence agencies—what some call the 'deep state' but which he describes as simply the same people rotating through a 'revolving door.' He contends that journalism has been fully captured by this regime and now functions as propaganda. Rather than violent revolution, he proposes creating a rigorous, fact-based documentation project to expose the actual structure of power, believing that honest transparency would undermine the regime's ability to maintain control through obfuscation.
key_points: The actual power structure differs fundamentally from what's publicly presented—it's an interconnected oligarchy operating through revolving doors between government, corporations, media, and other institutions
Modern journalism has been 'captured' and functions as regime propaganda rather than serving as an independent fourth estate
The regime maintains power primarily through lying about its own identity and structure
Revolution against this system requires documentation and truth rather than physical confrontation, since 'there is no Bastille' to storm
Any effort to document power must be rigorously factual, non-editorializing, and avoid pushing agendas to maintain credibility
money_quotes: At some point in history, we stopped being citizens of a free society and became something for the ruling class to manage.
As much as there is consent, it is manufactured. You work to make money, they print the money.
While they don't present as 'official government propaganda outlet', they are fully captured by the regime and function completely in that regard.
The regime keeps power by lying about its identity. I don't believe if it was honest about itself it would continue to exist.
But the world has nothing I want, and that's the difference. I don't want money, I don't want love, I don't want respect, and I don't want the pathetic thing that passes for power today.
I want to live forever. I want fully immersive VR tech. I want to meet superhuman intelligences. I want to go to Mars (but only when they have 5 star hotels).
This is the most insidious evil power structure, because it's nowhere you can point to, and spends a lot of its energy lying about its structure.
I believe this regime is cold and evil, and it will wither in the warm light of truth.
key_insight: The modern power structure maintains control not through visible force but through obscuring its own nature, making rigorous documentation of who actually holds power a more revolutionary act than any protest.
